posts.text:
Released Rackula v26.5.0 and you might be wondering "hey, wasn't it on like v0.10??" - and you're right! we just switched to Calendar Versioning (CalVer), so I don't have to overthink between a major, minor or patch release. Also, because biggest number is best number.

Also includes some prep work for an upcoming Proxmox Community Scripts LXC deployment method.

Changelog: https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v26.5.0

Dev thriends: what are your thoughts on Calendar Versioning (CalVer) vs the traditional Semantic Versioning (SemVer)? I ask because I'm leaning towards switching Rackula to CalVer, specifically YY. MM. MICRO format. My reasoning is it removes some of the annoying friction I'm finding around deciding what is a minor vs major release on SemVer style releasing. The other thought I have is that because Rackula isn't a library, the actual version number is less important than release date freshness

Released Rackula v0.10.1 with a few bug fixes and adjustments. Web on Mobile with SVGs is hard! ...anyways, you can click on things now, so that's nice. 

https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v0.10.1
